<!-- Generated by the mad-codebase-audit agent fleet, 2026-06-15. 44 findings raised, 35 confirmed after adversarial verification (55 agents). Working-tree audit (uncommitted session work included), read-only. NO auto-fixing was done. -->

# MAD Audit — Prioritized Findings

All findings below were adversarially verified as real. Severities reflect the verifier's corrected ratings (which in several cases differ from the original).

## Critical

None. No finding in this audit causes unrecoverable user-data loss or a crash on a normal path with no mitigation.

## Should-fix

These produce a real wrong behavior, a broken safety contract, or a violation of a hard project rule — but each is recoverable or narrow in scope.

**1. `profiles.apply_slot` writes the live Cemu/Eden controller config with no running-emulator (EBUSY) guard**
`lib/madsrv/backends_cmds.py:207`, `lib/mad_backup.py:48` (cemu copy :69, eden rewrite :81).
Cemu/Eden rewrite their controller config on exit, so applying a slot profile while the emulator is open lets the emulator clobber the just-applied profile (or race a live read). This is the lone config writer missing the EBUSY guard that every sibling has — and the very same module's `do_restore` (mad_backup.py:119-124) already refuses for this exact reason.
Fix: call `proc_guard.emulator_running(bname)` at the top of `apply_slot` (both "cemu" and "eden" are already in `EMULATOR_PROCS`) and raise `RpcError('EBUSY', 'Close <emu> first…')`.

**2. The Eden path of `apply_slot_profile` writes `qt-config.ini` non-atomically and skips the staterev bump**
`lib/mad_backup.py:81`.
A bare `ini.write_text(...)` on the live Eden config — a crash mid-write can truncate it, and because it bypasses `fsutil.atomic_write`, `staterev("config")` never fires so MAD's page cache can serve stale data. The sibling `eden_cfg.py:199-200/266-267` does the identical section-write but correctly routes through `fsutil.atomic_write`.
Fix: route this write through `fsutil.atomic_write` (already imported, mad_backup.py:21) — one line. (Note: the "not byte-preserving" framing in the original finding is inaccurate; Eden writers are section-rewriters by design. The real issues are atomicity + the staterev bump.)

**3. `model2.set` has no EBUSY guard before rewriting `EMULATOR.INI`**
`lib/madsrv/model2_cmds.py:198`; `lib/proc_guard.py:87-98` (no model2/m2emu entry).
m2emu reads `EMULATOR.INI` at start and rewrites it on exit, so a panel edit made while the game is open is silently reverted. This is the only settings writer with no running-emulator guard; even the sibling Sega-arcade page (model3/Supermodel) guards. Confirmed by `deck-docs/model2-emulator-ini.md:59` ("the emu rewrites these").
Fix: detect m2emu via `pgrep -f EMULATOR.EXE` (the launcher execs that under umu/Proton, same `pgrep -f` style eden/ryujinx use) and refuse with EBUSY while running.

**4. Eden `qt-config.ini` has two independent writers with two different one-time backups → the ".bak" snapshot is not guaranteed pristine**
`lib/eden_cfg.py:194` & `:263` (`.router-backup`) vs `lib/madsrv/cfgutil.py:42` (`.bak`).
The device-assignment writer and the settings writer each take their own "one-time original" backup of the same file. Whichever runs second snapshots an already-modified file, so that backup is not the user's true original. (The two writers touch disjoint sections, so the blast radius is bounded — the verifier downgraded this to cleanup; included here as a should-fix-adjacent contract defect, treat per your appetite.)
Fix: have both Eden writers share one ensure-once backup path/helper.

**5. `do_backup` merges into the persistent snapshot dir (`dirs_exist_ok=True`), so a "true restore" can resurrect deleted files**
`lib/mad_backup.py:90` (merge at :98), contradicting `do_restore`'s docstring (`:110`, exact-mirror restore at :137/153/170).
`do_backup` reuses the persistent `SNAP_DIR` and never clears stale entries for directory targets (e.g. Cemu `controllerProfiles`). Backup with file X → delete X from live → re-backup (X lingers in the stale snapshot) → restore resurrects X, violating `do_restore`'s explicit "no resurrecting files you deleted" guarantee. Dir-targets only; resurrected items are stale config, not user data.
Fix: retire/clear `snap/name` (via `recoverable_delete`) before the `copytree` so the snapshot is a true point-in-time mirror.

**6. Standalone launches (Wii U / Xbox / Switch / Wii) show the X-Arcade warning dialog TWICE**
`ES-DE/scripts/game-start/04-controller-router-setup.sh:21` + `05-controller-router-standalone.sh:26`; `controller-router.py:220` and `:324`.
Both game-start hooks fire; the 04 hook only self-skips wrapped commands, and unwrapped standalone consoles reach `_xarcade_warn` in both `_setup` and `_standalone`. When only the X-Arcade is connected the user gets two sequential blocking tkinter dialogs for one launch. (Correction to the original finding: **ps3 is NOT affected** — its command IS wrapped, so 04 skips it. Affected: wiiu, xbox, switch, wii.)
Fix: have `_setup` return early for standalone systems before the warn block, or have the 04 hook skip any `is_standalone` command (05 owns standalone warnings).

**7. `controller-policy.local.toml` flips Switch `router_skip` to false, defeating the documented hands-off intent**
`controller-policy.local.toml:82` vs base `controller-policy.toml:224`; merge at `routing.py:38-42`; live handler at `controller-router.py:372-374`.
The GUI-written local override flips the protective `router_skip=true` to false; `deep_merge` makes local win. This is **not** harmless — `_standalone` falls through to an active `eden_assign` handler that rewrites Eden's hand-configured Switch input on every Switch launch, violating the documented "must NOT touch Switch input" rule. (Verifier raised this from cleanup to should-fix; the original finding's "saved by no standalone action" claim is wrong — the handler exists.)
Fix: remove the `[systems.switch] router_skip = false` block from the local toml, or have the GUI never write `router_skip=false` for switch.

**8. `wrap_system` silently drops intra-`<system>` comments and any unhandled child element when re-rendering a CUSTOM system**
`lib/es_systems_wrap.py:66` (`_render_block` re-emits only name/fullname/path/extension/command/platform/theme).
Re-wrapping a curated custom system (e.g. dreamcast, which has an inline explanatory comment at custom_systems/es_systems.xml ~lines 141-143) discards that comment and any future ES-DE `<system>` child — exactly the loss the module's docstring claims text-splicing avoids. **Latent today**: no production caller of `wrap_system` was found anywhere (Python, RPC, or C++), so impact is zero until it is wired.
Fix: when src is from CUSTOM, splice the command in-place on that block, or carry through unknown children/inner comments.

**9. `deck-restore.sh` extracts the config archive over `$HOME` with no `_TMP` backup of the live config it overwrites**
`deck-restore.sh:60-67`.
Violates CLAUDE.md rule 5. The archive holds absolute paths (~/ES-DE gamelists/settings, launchers, EmuDeck config, Claude memory, both AppImages) and is unpacked over the live tree after a single y/N prompt. Restoring a stale archive onto a working Deck silently and unrecoverably replaces newer data. `deck-fetch-esde.sh:121-131` and `install.sh:64` already use the `_TMP-…-<ts>` + RECOVERY.txt pattern; this path skips it.
Fix: snapshot the paths the archive will touch into a same-filesystem `_TMP-restore-<ts>/` with RECOVERY.txt before the extract, and print the path.

**10. `install.sh` uses `pacman -Sy` (partial-upgrade footgun) while `deck-post-update.sh` uses `pacman -S` for the same packages**
`install.sh:180` vs `deck-post-update.sh:220` (`python-evdev tk`).
`-Sy pkg` refreshes the DB without a full `-Su`, the classic Arch partial-upgrade hazard: it can pull packages built against a newer python/libc than the frozen SteamOS root, breaking the very tkinter/evdev import the step provides. (Verifier rated best-practice, medium confidence — the two aren't strictly interchangeable: `pacman-key --init/--populate` doesn't fetch the sync DB, so plain `-S` on a never-refreshed root could fail "target not found", which likely explains the `-Sy` on the first-time installer. Defensible tension, not a clear bug.)
Fix: align to `-S` if you confirm the DB is populated at that point; otherwise document why the installer needs `-Sy`.

**11. `GuiMadPageGamePicker` doesn't refresh the "• custom" override badge after editing a per-game override (missing `onChildPopped`)**
`es-app/src/guis/mad/pages/GuiMadPageGamePicker.cpp:64`.
The picker builds per-game buttons with a "• custom" badge but never overrides `onChildPopped`, so after a user creates a *first* per-game override and backs out, the game still shows without the badge until they leave and re-enter. The sibling `GuiMadPageSystems::onChildPopped` (GuiMadPageSystems.cpp:27-33) was written for exactly this. (Scope: **Ryujinx-specific** — Eden refuses to create new overrides from MAD, so its badge is never stale.)
Fix: add `onChildPopped()` that re-issues the `<ns>.games` request (refactor `build`'s request into a `requestGames()` helper), preserving the focus cursor.

## Cleanup

Genuine duplication, dead code, or stale config/comments. No behavioral bug today; maintenance/divergence hazards.

**Backend duplication**
- **`systems_cmds._retroarch_running` duplicates `proc_guard.retroarch_running`** — `lib/madsrv/systems_cmds.py:34` (used :237). `proc_guard.py`'s docstring explicitly names this as a copy to canonicalize, but the module never imports proc_guard. Fix: import proc_guard, drop the local def.
- **`pcsx2_cfg` reimplements `inifile.section_body`/`set_section` verbatim** — `lib/pcsx2_cfg.py:83-98` vs `lib/inifile.py:14-29`. xemu_cfg/eden_cfg already import these; the copies have a latent empty-body divergence. Fix: import from inifile.
- **Four pad-assignment backends duplicate the same select/prioritize/pin/fallback algorithm** — `lib/pcsx2_cfg.py:115`, `xemu_cfg.py:40`, `eden_cfg.py:113`, `rpcs3_cfg.py:90` (identical prio/ps lines at pcsx2:136-138 / xemu:62-64 / eden:134-136 / rpcs3:111-113). The pin-collision drop loops already differ subtly between files. Fix: extract a shared helper returning `{slot: device-or-None}`; backends supply only the value-encoding callback.
- **`KNOWN_PADS` and `PAD_SHORT` duplicate the same vid:pid label map** — `lib/mad_config.py:16-26`. Both live; a new pad must be added in two places. Fix: derive `PAD_SHORT` from `KNOWN_PADS` plus the synthetic x-arcade entry. (Dimension mislabeled "dead-code"; it's duplication.)
- **Single-hop `inherits` backend resolution reimplemented twice, diverging from `routing.resolve_system`'s full-chain resolver** — `lib/es_systems.py:121-122` & `lib/mad_config.py:145-146` follow one hop; `routing.py:81-104` walks the full chain. `_resolve_backend`'s docstring falsely claims it "mirrors the router." **Latent only** — the verifier computed every system in the live policy and found zero divergence (all backend-carrying systems set `backend` directly). Fix: build both on `routing.resolve_system(...).get('backend')`.

**Backend dead code / redundancy**
- **`ryujinx.set` creates TWO backups (`.bak` + `.router-backup`) for the same config** — `lib/madsrv/ryujinx_cmds.py:164-165`. Inconsistent with ryujinx input/cfg writers (which create only `.router-backup`); the `.bak` of a freshly-cloned per-game file backs up nothing. Fix: drop the `ensure_bak` call.
- **`joypad_indices` is dead on the live RetroArch routing path** — `controller-router.py:246`, never assigned, always empty/None; plumbing in `retroarch_cfg.py:188-242` is unreachable from production (reverted feature #37). Fix: remove the local + guard term + arg, or drop the param.

**C++ dead state / duplication**
- **`GuiMadPagePadsPriority::mListCookie` written in `onSaveFocus` but never read** — `GuiMadPagePadsPriority.cpp:349` (declared .h:68). Reorder-list cursor reverts to index 0 on restore; restore half never wired. Low impact (page pushes no children). Fix: restore it, or delete it.
- **`GuiMadPagePadsPriority::mPlayers` written, never read** — `GuiMadPagePadsPriority.cpp:25/58`. Parsed from the RPC payload but unused (player slots resolved backend-side). Fix: drop it or use it to label P1..Pn rows.
- **`GuiMadPageSystemDetail`/Systems duplicates the toggle-row + roll-back-on-failure pattern twice** — `GuiMadPageSystems.cpp:223-280` (loops), `:287-329`/`:331-364` (setFlag vs setRaOption). Two parallel structs + two near-identical writers for one concept. Fix: factor one `applyToggle(method, keyField, …)` helper.
- **`MadSidebar`/`MadPlayerSlots` lack the degenerate-clip guard their siblings document as required** — `MadSidebar.cpp:107`, `MadPlayerSlots.cpp:321` vs `MadTileGrid.cpp:234`/`MadScrollView.cpp:81`. A zero-rounded dim disables clipping (Renderer.cpp:429-432), so rows/icons could bleed past the panel. Purely latent on the Deck's fixed 1280×800 layout. Fix: add `if (clipDim.x < 1 || clipDim.y < 1) return;` before `pushClipRect`.

**Cross-cutting / config / theme**
- **`deck-post-update.sh --check` health gate verifies the RETIRED Tk path, not live C++ panel reachability** — `deck-post-update.sh:37` (used by esde-health-check.sh every launch). The fast path checks `import tkinter, evdev` but never asserts `mad-backend.py --selfcheck`. (Nuance: tkinter is NOT dead — warning_dialog.py:39 and the Desktop-mode router-config-gui.py still need it; the accurate gap is the *missing backend selfcheck*, narrower than the title.) Fix: also gate on the backend selfcheck the full run does.
- **Dead RPC methods left from the retired Tk GUI** — `preview.route` (preview_cmds.py:188), `policy.gui_flags/set_gui_flag/local/reset_local/set_backend_template`, `esde.systems`, `devices.battery/sdl`. Exhaustive grep confirms 0 C++ callers and no internal Python dispatch. Fix: delete or clearly mark Desktop-Tk-only.
- **Stale comment: PS2 default is PCSX2 standalone, not the LRPS2 RA core** — `controller-policy.toml:269`. es_systems.xml:267 makes PCSX2 standalone the first/default command; no `<alternativeEmulator>` set. Dual routing is fine; only the comment misleads. Fix: correct the comment.
- **`launchers/art/icons` missing 6 icons present in the theme's `router-config/icons`** — `lib/madsrv/systems_cmds.py:48-74` resolve chain. eden/ryujinx (standalones_cmds.py:59,61) lose their icon if the custom theme is replaced, since the repo fallback layer has no copy. Fix: copy the 6 theme-only icons into launchers/art/icons, or document router-config as the single source.
- **`pixel.xml` is a byte-identical orphaned duplicate of `theme.xml`** — `themes/pixel-es-de/pixel.xml` (md5 matches theme.xml; never an include target except the also-orphaned genh/theme-new_bg.xml). Future theme.xml edits silently diverge. Fix: move pixel.xml to `_TMP` per rule 5.
- **`router-config/theme.xml` references a missing `./console.png` (dead ES-DE system theme)** — `themes/pixel-es-de/router-config/theme.xml:16`. No system maps to theme name "router-config", so it's never loaded; the folder is an intentional MAD sidecar (theme.toml documents ES-DE ignores it). Trivial leftover-template cleanup, not a "broken theme." Fix: drop the stray theme.xml (the rest of the sidecar dir stays).
- **`deck-restore.sh` emulator-detection globs match the restored data dir, not an installed emulator** — `deck-restore.sh:123` (`app:ryujinx*`, `app:Vita3K*`). Suffixless globs match a directory (verified: `~/Applications/Vita3K` is a dir) and falsely print `[ok]`; other rows use `*.AppImage`. Post-restore advisory only. Fix: tighten to the real artifact, or require an executable file in `emu_installed()`.

## Best-practice

Accepted tradeoffs or hardening; flagged for awareness, fix optional.

- **Cemu handheld/clear paths `unlink()` managed `controllerN.xml` instead of move-to-`_TMP`** — `lib/cemu_cfg.py:150` (called :198/215/228). Rule-5-adjacent: original is recoverable via the one-time `.router-backup`, but a user hand-edit made after MAD's first run is unlink()'d with no fresh backup. The codebase already ships `fsutil.recoverable_delete()` (fsutil.py:128) which cemu bypasses. Fix: route deletes through `recoverable_delete`, or refresh the backup if mtime is newer.
- **rpcs3 input writer does a whole-file YAML round-trip (strips comments/formatting)** — `lib/rpcs3_cfg.py:186`. RPCS3 owns Default.yml's format and rewrites it on exit, and the writer builds whole `Player N Input` blocks (not single-value edits), so cfgutil's byte-preserving rule doesn't really apply. Fix: document the intentional full-rewrite.
- **`MadBackend` destructor runs up to ~2s (worst-case ~4s) of blocking sleeps on the UI thread, reachable from `input()` via `delete this`** — `MadBackend.cpp:476-502`, called from `GuiMadPanel.cpp:451/461/482`. A wedged daemon turns a Back-press into a multi-second UI freeze before SIGKILL; a healthy daemon (EOF teardown) is near-instant. Fix: shorten the SIGTERM grace or SIGKILL sooner on the interactive teardown path, and document the worst case.
- **`capture.button` start failure: a bare `{closed}` push racing the response is silently dropped** — `MadBackend.cpp:360-374`, `GuiMadCaptureModal.cpp:80-91`. Reachable if `evdev.list_devices()` raises before any subscriber registers (the modal stays in the unarmed busy state; B-to-cancel still works, so it's not a true hang — the original "arms and hangs 15s" narrative is inaccurate). Fix: log when a bare closed push is dropped so a protocol regression is visible.

## Completeness check

Gaps and dimensions this audit did NOT cover or could not verify:

- **No on-device runtime verification.** Everything here is static code reading (the verifier ran a few Python helpers and `md5sum`/`compgen` checks, but launched nothing). No GUI page was rendered, no emulator launched, no router invoked end-to-end. Per CLAUDE.md the subagent has no display; the C++ focus/nav, render, and full launch→game→quit flows are unverified at runtime.
- **C++ memory/lifetime safety not audited.** The raw-pointer + `addChild(child.get())` pattern, page push/pop ownership, and component lifetimes (a known ES-DE footgun class) were only touched incidentally (the `delete this` finding). No systematic dangling-pointer / use-after-free pass was done.
- **RPC concurrency / daemon threading.** The `_ENUM_CACHE` lock, watch threads, stream lifecycle, and the page-cache staterev invariant were touched only where a specific finding landed (the apply_slot staterev miss). No broad audit of races between concurrent RPC calls, the watch thread, and config writers.
- **Writer coverage is incomplete.** EBUSY/atomicity/backup was checked across the named writers, but `supermodel`/model3, `ryujinx_input`, dolphin, and the `*.sh` per-emulator launchers were only spot-checked. There may be other writers with the same guard/backup gaps.
- **Shell launchers (~60 scripts) largely unexamined.** Only deck-backup/deck-restore/install/deck-post-update and the game-start/game-end hooks were inspected. Quoting/`set -euo pipefail`/path-injection across the remaining launchers, wrap.sh scripts, and `mad-switch-launch.py` were not audited.
- **`controller-policy.toml` semantic correctness** beyond the switch/ps2 items was not fully validated (backend mappings, category defaults, Sinden/lightgun routing correctness per system).
- **No security review** (no audit of `sudo`/`pacman`/`steamos-readonly` toggles beyond the `-Sy` finding, RPC input trust boundaries, or the capture subsystem's evdev handling for malformed input).
- **Theme/art** was checked only for orphans/missing-fallback; no validation that the live MAD theme renders correctly or that the madTheme color contract matches what the C++ pages expect.
- **No build verification** — none of the suggested C++ changes were compiled; the ES-DE fork was read, not built.
- **Latent-vs-live ambiguity** on several findings (es_systems_wrap unwired, single-hop resolver, degenerate-clip, deep inherits chains): confirmed correct in code but their real-world trigger depends on future config/wiring, so "impact today = none" rests on the current on-disk state, which can change.
